Spectral Data API

EchelleBase.SpectralData.SpecData — Type

An abstract type for all spectral data, both 2d echellograms and extracted 1d spectra, parametrized by the spectrograph S.

EchelleBase.SpectralData.SpecData1d — Type

A SpecData1d.

Fields

  • fname: The starting pixel.
  • header: The ending pixel.
  • data: DataFrame.
EchelleBase.SpectralData.SpecData2d — Type

An abstract type for all 2d spectral data (echellograms), parametrized by the spectrograph S.

Missing docstring.

Missing docstring for Echellogram. Check Documenter's build log for details.

Missing docstring.

Missing docstring for RawSpecData2d. Check Documenter's build log for details.

Missing docstring.

Missing docstring for MasterCal2d. Check Documenter's build log for details.

EchelleBase.SpectralData.get_spectrograph — Function
get_spectrograph(data::SpecData{S})

Returns the name of the spectrograph as a string corresponding to this SpecData object.

EchelleBase.SpectralData.get_spec_module — Function
get_spec_module(data::SpecData{S})

Returns the module for this spectrograph.

EchelleBase.SpectralRegions.SpecRegion1d — Type

A container for 1d spectral regions for reduced spectra.

Fields

  • pixmin: The starting pixel.
  • pixmax: The ending pixel.
  • λmin: The starting wavelength.
  • λmax: The ending wavelength.
  • order: The echelle order number, if applicable.
  • label: The label for this chunk.
  • fiber: The fiber number, if applicable.
EchelleBase.SpectralRegions.SpecRegion2d — Type

A container for 2d spectral regions for echellograms.

Fields

  • pixmin: The starting pixel in the spectral direction.
  • pixmax: The ending pixel in the spectral direction.
  • orderbottom: The bottom order.
  • ordertop: The top order.
  • poly_bottom: The bottom bounding polynomial.
  • poly_top: The top bounding polynomial.
EchelleBase.mask! — Function
mask!(data::SpecData1d, sregion::SpecRegion1d)

Mask bad pixels in-place according to the variables data.data.λ, data.data.flux, data.data.fluxerr, data.data.mask, as well as the bounding pixels in sregion (not wavelength).

mask!(image::AbstractMatrix{<:Number}, sregion::SpecRegion2d)

Mask bad pixels in-place according to the bounding polynomials and left/right ends.
